# The Future of Veterinary Anatomic Pathology: Integrating Technology for Better Outcomes

## Blog

### **Introduction**

Veterinary medicine is witnessing a paradigm shift, particularly in the realm of anatomic pathology. The integration of technology into veterinary diagnostic practices has sparked notable improvements in accuracy and efficiency. In this blog post, we delve into “Veterinary Anatomic Pathology Trends,” exploring how modern technology is transforming the landscape of veterinary diagnostics.

### **Evolution of Veterinary Anatomic Pathology**

Traditionally, veterinary anatomic pathology relied on conventional slide-based microscopy and manual analysis. However, the growing complexity of animal diseases and the need for faster diagnostics have driven the need for technological integration. Today, digital tools, telepathology, and advanced imaging techniques are revolutionizing veterinary pathology, providing faster and more reliable diagnostic outcomes.

### **Technological Innovations Driving Change**

#### **Digital Imaging and Telepathology**

Digital imaging systems are at the forefront of these trends. High-resolution scanners allow for the digitization of veterinary tissue samples, enabling remote analysis and consultations. Telepathology facilitates instant collaboration among specialists, ensuring that even complex cases can benefit from collective expertise regardless of geographical boundaries.

#### **Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning**

The incorporation of AI and machine learning into veterinary pathology is an emerging trend. These technologies can analyze digital images for patterns and anomalies that may be subtle or overlooked by the human eye. This support system not only increases diagnostic accuracy but also serves as an educational tool for veterinary pathologists in training.

#### **Integration with Laboratory Information Systems**

Modern veterinary labs are increasingly integrating digital pathology data into centralized [laboratory information systems (LIS)](/blog/streamlined-laboratory-operations/). This integration streamlines workflow, reduces manual errors, and ensures that data is easily accessible for future reference or research. The trend toward a more connected and data-driven approach is transforming how veterinary pathology is practiced.

### **Benefits of Embracing Digital Trends in Veterinary Pathology**

#### **Enhanced Diagnostic Precision**

By utilizing advanced digital tools and AI-driven analytics, veterinary pathology can achieve higher levels of precision. This is crucial for diagnosing complex diseases in animals, where early and accurate detection can significantly improve treatment outcomes.

#### **Improved Efficiency and Collaboration**

Digital workflows allow veterinary labs to handle larger case volumes without compromising on quality. With remote consultations and collaborative platforms, veterinary pathologists can share insights and expertise more readily, leading to faster and more informed diagnostic decisions.

#### **Cost-Effective and Scalable Solutions**

Although the initial investment in digital tools can be high, the long-term benefits include cost savings through reduced labor, faster turnaround times, and fewer repeat tests. As these technologies become more widespread, the cost per case is expected to decline, making them more accessible to a wider range of veterinary practices.

### **Addressing the Challenges**

#### **Infrastructure and Training**

One of the primary challenges is the need for robust digital infrastructure. Veterinary labs must invest in high-speed internet, secure data storage, and compatible imaging systems. Additionally, staff training is critical; veterinary professionals need to be proficient in using new digital tools and interpreting digital images to ensure a smooth transition from traditional methods.

#### **Regulatory and Quality Assurance**

Veterinary labs must also navigate the evolving regulatory landscape surrounding digital data. Ensuring compliance with data protection laws and maintaining quality assurance standards is essential to protect patient information and uphold diagnostic accuracy.

### **Case Examples and Future Directions**

Several veterinary institutions have already embraced these trends with promising results. For example, a leading veterinary teaching hospital recently implemented a digital imaging system, reporting improved diagnostic turnaround times and enhanced collaborative efforts among its pathologists. Such case examples highlight the transformative potential of technology in veterinary anatomic pathology.

Looking ahead, we can expect further advancements in imaging technologies, AI-driven diagnostic tools, and enhanced data integration techniques. These developments will continue to shape “Veterinary Anatomic Pathology Trends,” ultimately leading to better outcomes for animal health care.

### **Conclusion**

The integration of modern technology into veterinary anatomic pathology is redefining the field. With innovations in digital imaging, AI, and data integration, veterinary labs are better equipped to deliver accurate, timely, and efficient diagnoses. These trends not only enhance the quality of veterinary care but also pave the way for future advancements in animal health diagnostics.

We are raising the bar with one-of-a-kind capabilities helping labs accession, diagnose and generate fully customizable reports faster.

### Ramesh Mhatale

#### Market Research Analyst

 Published Apr 28, 2025 

[+ Follow](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=%2Fpulse%2Fnavigating-future-veterinary-imaging-market-trends-drivers-mhatale-mjppe&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_publisher-author-card)

The veterinary imaging market has undergone a remarkable transformation in recent years, solidifying its position as an indispensable component of modern animal healthcare. Valued at two billion United States dollars in 2022, this market is on track to expand at a compound annual growth rate of 6.9 percent between 2023 and 2031, ultimately exceeding three point six billion dollars by the end of the forecast period. Such robust growth underscores the increasing recognition of advanced diagnostic imaging as a cornerstone for timely and accurate treatment planning, not only for companion animals but also for livestock and wildlife. As pet ownership rises globally and the economic stakes of animal disease outbreaks become more pronounced, veterinary professionals and institutions are investing heavily in technologies once reserved for human healthcare. The promise of earlier detection, minimally invasive procedures, and improved patient outcomes continues to drive the veterinary imaging landscape into exciting new territory.

Explore our report to gain an in-depth view of the key findings -

[https://www.transparencymarketresearch.com/veterinary-imaging-market.html](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Etransparencymarketresearch%2Ecom%2Fveterinary-imaging-market%2Ehtml&urlhash=P-Nv&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_little-text-block)

Market Overview and Key Drivers

At the heart of this expansion lies a confluence of factors that are reshaping the veterinary imaging industry. First and foremost, research and development investments in the pharmaceutical and diagnostic sectors have accelerated the evolution of imaging hardware and software solutions. Multimodal imaging devices that combine radiography, ultrasound, and even magnetic resonance imaging capabilities in a single platform are becoming commonplace, enabling veterinarians to obtain comprehensive diagnostic data with greater efficiency. Furthermore, the integration of digital imaging and artificial intelligence is providing novel opportunities for automated lesion detection, three-dimensional reconstruction, and predictive analytics, all of which enhance diagnostic precision while reducing the risk of human error. Equally important is the mounting awareness among pet owners of the benefits associated with early diagnosis and preventive care. As a result, veterinary practices are under growing pressure to adopt state-of-the-art imaging modalities to meet client expectations and to differentiate themselves in an increasingly competitive marketplace.

Product Segment Analysis: Equipment, Reagents, and Software

The veterinary imaging market can be broadly segmented into three categories: equipment, imaging reagents, and software. In 2022, the equipment segment accounted for the lion’s share of revenue, reflecting the enduring demand for X-ray systems, ultrasound units, computed tomography scanners, magnetic resonance imaging machines, and endoscopy suites tailored for animals. Pricing strategies vary widely, allowing smaller clinics to acquire portable or refurbished systems at lower price points while enabling large hospitals to invest in high-end, floor-mounted installations. Meanwhile, the imaging reagents segment—comprising contrast agents and radiopharmaceuticals—continues to grow steadily as clinicians rely on these compounds to enhance soft tissue differentiation and vascular imaging. On the software side, vendors are rolling out applications that facilitate image archiving, teleconsultation, and artificial intelligence-driven analysis, further streamlining workflows and expanding the reach of expert interpretation to remote or underserved regions.

Animal Type Insights: Small Animals Leading the Charge

Companion animals—particularly dogs and cats—dominated the veterinary imaging market in 2022, accounting for the largest share of utilization. The surge in pet ownership rates, especially in urbanized areas, has fostered a culture of heightened pet welfare, prompting owners to seek the best possible diagnostic and therapeutic care for their animals. Consequently, imaging modalities such as digital radiography, high-frequency ultrasound, and low-field magnetic resonance imaging systems designed for small patients have witnessed exceptional uptake. Smaller form factors, specialized coils, and software presets calibrated for canine and feline anatomy enable veterinarians to perform comprehensive examinations without the logistical challenges posed by larger machines. This trend has also spurred equipment manufacturers to develop modular, multifunctional platforms that can be easily reconfigured for different animal sizes, further enhancing the versatility and cost-effectiveness of veterinary imaging solutions.

[![Image 6: The Role of Technology in the Evolution of Veterinary Care](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/navigating-future-veterinary-imaging-market-trends-drivers-mhatale-mjppe) The Role of Technology in the Evolution of Veterinary… Joe Flower 7 months ago](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/role-technology-evolution-veterinary-care-joe-flower-mup7c)

Application Segmentation: Orthopedics at the Forefront

Within the broader veterinary imaging spectrum, orthopedic applications have emerged as a primary growth driver. The prevalence of musculoskeletal conditions—ranging from traumatic fractures to chronic joint disorders such as osteoarthritis—has escalated in both companion and production animals. Radiography remains the workhorse for initial bone assessments, while computed tomography provides high-resolution, cross-sectional views critical for surgical planning. Magnetic resonance imaging, although less ubiquitous due to higher costs and longer scan times, offers unparalleled soft-tissue contrast, making it indispensable for diagnosing ligament injuries, spinal cord lesions, and complex musculoskeletal pathologies. Market players are capitalizing on this demand by introducing specialized orthopedic imaging packages, including 3D reconstruction software and weight-bearing radiography systems, designed to facilitate minimally invasive interventions and postoperative monitoring.

Regional Dynamics: North America’s Leading Role

Geographically, North America—led by the United States—continues to dominate the global veterinary imaging market. The region benefits from a well-established veterinary healthcare infrastructure, high per-capita pet expenditure, and progressive regulatory frameworks that encourage technology adoption. In addition, the presence of leading imaging manufacturers and research institutions has fostered a vibrant ecosystem for product innovation and clinical validation. Europe and Asia Pacific are also witnessing rapid growth, driven by expanding middle-class populations, rising disposable incomes, and increasing government initiatives to modernize veterinary services. In emerging markets such as India, Brazil, and parts of the Middle East, strategic partnerships and distribution agreements are helping to bridge the technology gap, enabling smaller practices to access advanced imaging capabilities through cost-effective leasing and mobile imaging solutions.

Competitive Landscape and Strategic Developments

The veterinary imaging sector is characterized by a relatively consolidated competitive landscape, with major healthcare conglomerates and specialized medical device firms vying for market share. Industry heavyweights such as 3M, Johnson & Johnson, Medtronic, Siemens Healthineers, GE Healthcare, Koninklijke Philips, Shenzhen Mindray, and Samsung are at the forefront, leveraging their extensive research and development pipelines as well as global distribution networks. Recent strategic initiatives underscore the momentum in the market: for example, GE Healthcare’s January 2023 distribution agreement with Sound Technologies to deliver the Vscan Air wireless ultrasound to veterinary practices exemplifies the trend toward portable, user-friendly imaging devices. Similarly, OmniVision’s November 2022 launch of a high-resolution endoscopic image sensor tailored for veterinary applications highlights the role of component-level innovation in driving new product introductions.

Future Outlook and Opportunities

Looking forward, the veterinary imaging market is poised for sustained expansion as technological advancements continue to blur the lines between human and animal diagnostics. Artificial intelligence algorithms trained on large veterinary image datasets hold promise for automating routine measurements, flagging subtle pathologies, and even predicting disease progression. The advent of cloud-based imaging platforms will facilitate seamless collaboration among veterinary specialists and foster telemedicine services in rural or underserved communities. Moreover, the growing focus on preventive and wellness care for aging pets is likely to spur demand for routine screening protocols, further embedding imaging services into standard veterinary practice. As manufacturers, service providers, and research institutions collaborate to refine imaging modalities and reduce total cost of ownership, the veterinary imaging market will undoubtedly remain a fertile ground for innovation and strategic growth.

Conclusion

The global veterinary imaging market is entering a new era defined by cross-disciplinary innovation, rising animal welfare expectations, and expanding applications across species. With the market projected to grow from two billion United States dollars in 2022 to more than three point six billion dollars by 2031, stakeholders across the value chain have a compelling opportunity to shape the future of animal healthcare. By embracing digital transformation, fostering collaborative research, and prioritizing client education, veterinary professionals and industry players can ensure that advanced imaging technologies translate into better outcomes for animals and sustainable growth for the market as a whole.

# Veterinary Imaging Market Size to Soar Amid Rising Pet Healthcare Awareness

The global veterinary imaging market is projected to grow from USD 2.10 billion in 2025 to USD 3.86 billion by 2034, expanding at a CAGR of 7.01% during the forecast period. This growth is propelled by increasing pet adoption, demand for early disease diagnosis, and advancements in imaging technology such as AI-powered diagnostics and portable radiology equipment.

![cart](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/assets/images/download.webp)
![cart](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/assets/images/list.webp)

## List of Contents

## Veterinary Imaging Market Size and Forecast 2025 to 2034

The global veterinary Imaging market size accounted at USD 1.96 billion in 2024 and is anticipated to reach around USD 3.86 billion by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 7.01% over the forecast period 2025 to 2034.

![Veterinary Imaging Market Size 2025 to 2034](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/insightimg/veterinary-imaging-market-size.webp "Veterinary Imaging Market Size 2025 to 2034")

## Veterinary Imaging Market Key Takeaways

## **What is the role of AI in the Veterinary Imaging Market?**

[Artificial intelligence](../artificial-intelligence-market) (AI) in[veterinary medicine](../veterinary-medicine-market)is an emerging field. [Machine learning](../machine-learning-market) (ML) is a subfield of artificial intelligence (AI) that enables computer programs to examine large imaging datasets and to learn to perform veterinary diagnostics imaging. AI in veterinary imaging plays an important role in filling the gaps and providing important information for evaluating illness and injury and for routine prevention. The benefits also include accurate, precise, and faster diagnosis and help to improve care.

## U.S. Veterinary Imaging Market Size and Growth 2025 to 2034

The U.S. veterinary Imaging market size was accounted at USD 600 million in 2024 and is projected to be worth around USD 1,210 million by 2034, poised to grow at a CAGR of 7.27% from 2025 to 2034.

![U.S. Veterinary Imaging Market Size 2025 to 2034](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/insightimg/us-veterinary-imaging-market-size.webp "U.S. Veterinary Imaging Market Size 2025 to 2034")

North America dominated the veterinary imaging market in 2024. The United States and Canada are the leading countries for the growth of the market in the North American region. The increasing demand for veterinary healthcare services is due to the increased pet population. In the United States, many animals suffer from different diseases, such as an increase in orthopedics conditions and incidences contributing to the market’s growth.

![Veterinary Imaging Market Share, By Region, 2024 (%)](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/insightimg/veterinary-imaging-market-share-by-region.webp "Veterinary Imaging Market Share, By Region, 2024 (%)")

Asia Pacific is estimated to be significantly growing during the forecast period of 2025-2034. The rising prevalence of animal disorders, increasing livestock population, rising animal health vigilance, increasing health concerns, rising pet animals’ adoption, and increased healthcare spending on [veterinary services](../veterinary-services-market) and animal health are contributing to the growth of the veterinary imaging market.

### **Market Overview**

The veterinary imaging market is a highly specialized discipline that focuses on the development and deployment of an extensive array of imaging technologies that cater to the medical requirements of animals. Veterinary imaging is one of the most frequently used types of diagnostic testing in both large and small animal hospitals across the world. Imaging provides a high amount of information by non-invasive means and does not cause unacceptable discomfort to patients or alter disease processes.

## **Veterinary Imaging Market Growth Factors**

## **Market Dynamics**

### **Driver**

**Increasing pet adoption demand**

The increasing pet adoption demand and awareness for innovative, efficient, and high-quality products are pushing companies to improve their offspring, contributing to the growth of the veterinary imaging market. This demand from emerging economies and developed markets helps the growth of the market. Also, supportive government regulations and policies help the market’s growth.

### **Restraint**

**Limitations that investors and businesses need to consider**

The limitations that investors and businesses need to consider include technological disruptions, geopolitical tensions, and economic volatility that may impact the stability of the market, affecting business operations and investment returns. In some segments of the market, competition is great, which leads to market saturation, regulatory hurdles, etc., which can hamper the growth of the veterinary imaging market.

### **Opportunity**

**Advanced imaging technology**

The use of advanced imaging technologies like CT scans, MRIs, and [ultrasound](../ultrasound-market) contributes to the growth of the veterinary imaging market. Increasing investment in research and development and exposure to advanced imaging system technology help the market’s growth. The increasing prevalence of many veterinary disorders and the need for early and accurate diagnosis fueled the growth of veterinary imaging.

### **Product Insights**

The X-ray segment dominated the veterinary imaging market in 2024. In veterinary diagnosis, X-rays, also called radiographs, play an important role. They can provide a comprehensive image of the internal structures of animals, like abdominal organs, lungs, joints, and bones. This painless technique enables veterinarians to visualize potential foreign objects, tumors, fractures, and other abnormalities, which helps them understand many conditions accurately. It is a readily available and quick imaging method, enabling veterinarians to receive critical diagnostic information rapidly.

The video endoscopy segment is expected to be the fastest-growing during the forecast period. Video imaging has many benefits in terms of documentation, operator comfort, teaching, and client relations, which help the growth of the veterinary imaging market. Video endoscopy also allows endoscopists to work more effectively with any assistance who is supporting the process. The advanced and innovative video endoscopy includes minimally invasive procedures, video recording capabilities, high-resolution images, etc. The benefits of veterinary imaging video endoscopy for veterinarians include customer satisfaction, greater efficiency, a wide range of procedures, accurate diagnosis, exceptional customer service, competitive prices, superior quality, etc.

Ottomed Endoscopy provides veterinary video endoscopes for large and small animals with next-generation imaging technology, which is designed to help veterinary endoscopists enhance the [precision diagnostic](../precision-diagnostics-market) at the time of making diagnosis and therapeutic endoscopic treatments:

### **Solution Insights**

The equipment segment dominated the veterinary imaging market in 2024. The benefits of veterinary imaging equipment include saving money on costs, lower exposure, more accurate images, and seeing images that are clearer and more defined to reduce guesswork needed for accurate diagnosis making. The rapid imaging process is able to access images in an effective way, which helps to save time and also makes rapid diagnosis and treatment decisions in emergency situations. Veterinary imaging equipment can be used to analyze dental problems, evaluate soft tissue, determine bone issues, and more.

The PACS segment is anticipated to be the fastest-growing during the forecast period. The PACS solution plays an important role in improving animal care. The benefits of PACS for veterinary imaging include less time wasted because of inaccessible images and downtime; better patient diagnoses because of accurate diagnoses, and improved workflows for less frustration and stress for the staff, leading to less burnout and better outcomes.

### **Animal Type Insights**

The small animals segment dominated the veterinary imaging market in 2024, and it is estimated to be the fastest-growing segment during the forecast period. Small animals play an important role in veterinary imaging. Specific molecular target imaging with small animal PET allows earlier detection and characterization of diseases. The benefits of small animal veterinary imaging include screening of drug candidates, detailed biochemical parameters, complete kinetics, statistical benefits, lowered use of animals, etc., contributing to the market’s growth.

![Veterinary Imaging Market Share, by Animal Type, 2024 (%)](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/insightimg/veterinary-imaging-market-share-by-animal-type.webp "Veterinary Imaging Market Share, by Animal Type, 2024 (%)")

### **Application Insights**

The orthopedics and traumatology segment dominated the veterinary imaging market in 2024. Veterinary imaging plays an important role in the diagnosis and management of orthopedics and traumatology conditions. The benefits of veterinary imaging for orthopedics and traumatology include accurate diagnosis, treatment planning for soft tissue injuries, joint diseases, fractures, monitoring progress, etc.

The [oncology](../oncology-market) segment is expected to be the fastest-growing during the forecast period. Veterinary imaging plays an important role in adequate diagnosis and staging in human and veterinary oncology, contributing to the growth of the veterinary imaging market. Sensitive detection of lesions is essential to determine proper local and systematic therapy and to monitor therapeutic results.

### **End-use Insights**

The veterinary clinics and hospitals segment dominated the veterinary imaging market in 2024, and the segment is anticipated to be the fastest-growing during the forecast period. The benefits of veterinary clinics and hospitals include tax benefits for veterinary practices, potential practices for business expansion and franchising, fulfilling work that helps animals and their owners, potential to specialize and offer unique services, potential for repeat business and customer loyalty, recession-resistant industry because of pet ownership, steady income from pet care routine care, etc.

![Veterinary Imaging Market Share, by End-use, 2024 (%)](https://www.precedenceresearch.com/insightimg/veterinary-imaging-market-share-by-end-use.webp "Veterinary Imaging Market Share, by End-use, 2024 (%)")

enriched_points.expanded_content: Advancements in imaging technologies are revolutionizing veterinary diagnostics, integrating tools like AI and digital imaging for enhanced accuracy and efficiency in animal care.
Implementing ethical AI in veterinary imaging ensures responsible use, prioritizing animal welfare while enhancing diagnostic capabilities through automated analysis and pattern recognition.

Increased investment in innovative veterinary imaging solutions, including portable devices and cloud-based platforms, is driving market growth and improving accessibility for veterinary practices globally.
